title: FedRAMP Security Control Implementation Guide
content:
# FedRAMP Security Control Implementation Guide

## Overview

The Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) provides a standardized approach to security assessment, authorization, and continuous monitoring for cloud products and services used by federal agencies. FedRAMP builds on NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls and defines three impact levels (Low, Moderate, High) based on FIPS 199 categorization.

Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) seeking FedRAMP authorization must implement the required security controls, undergo assessment by a Third Party Assessment Organization (3PAO), and maintain continuous monitoring. SOC teams play a critical role in satisfying detection, response, and monitoring requirements.

## FedRAMP Impact Levels

| Impact Level | Control Count | Typical Use Case | Data Sensitivity |
|-------------|--------------|------------------|------------------|
| Low | ~156 controls | Publicly available data, low-impact SaaS | Loss would have limited adverse effect |
| Moderate | ~325 controls | Most federal data, PII, business-sensitive | Loss would have serious adverse effect |
| High | ~421 controls | Law enforcement, financial, health, critical infrastructure | Loss would have severe or catastrophic effect |

## Security Control Families

FedRAMP organizes controls into 20 families from NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5:

| Family | ID | Key Controls for SOC | Impact Level Focus |
|--------|----|---------------------|-------------------|
| Access Control | AC | AC-2, AC-3, AC-6, AC-7, AC-17 | All levels |
| Audit and Accountability | AU | AU-2, AU-3, AU-6, AU-7, AU-12 | All levels |
| Security Assessment | CA | CA-2, CA-7, CA-8 | Moderate and above |
| Configuration Management | CM | CM-2, CM-3, CM-6, CM-8 | All levels |
| Contingency Planning | CP | CP-2, CP-7, CP-9, CP-10 | Moderate and above |
| Identification and Authentication | IA | IA-2, IA-4, IA-5, IA-8 | All levels |
| Incident Response | IR | IR-2, IR-4, IR-5, IR-6, IR-8 | All levels |
| Risk Assessment | RA | RA-3, RA-5 | All levels |
| System and Communications Protection | SC | SC-7, SC-8, SC-12, SC-13, SC-28 | All levels |
| System and Information Integrity | SI | SI-2, SI-3, SI-4, SI-5 | All levels |

## Critical Controls for SOC Operations

### AU-2: Audit Events

**Requirement**: The information system must generate audit records for the following events:

| Event Category | Examples | Minimum Fields |
|---------------|----------|----------------|
| Authentication events | Successful/failed logon, logoff, session timeout | User ID, timestamp, source IP, success/failure |
| Authorization events | Access granted/denied, privilege changes | User ID, resource, action, result |
| Account management | Create, modify, disable, delete accounts | Admin ID, target account, action, timestamp |
| Configuration changes | System settings, security policy modifications | Admin ID, setting changed, old/new value |
| Process execution | Application starts, service changes | Process name, user context, command line |
| Network events | Connection establishment, firewall actions | Source/dest IP, port, protocol, action |

**FedRAMP Additional Requirements**:
- AU-2(d): Coordinate audit event selection across organizational systems
- Moderate/High: Include data creation, modification, and deletion events
- High: Include privileged function execution and information system component inventory changes

### AU-6: Audit Review, Analysis, and Reporting

**Requirements by Impact Level**:

| Requirement | Low | Moderate | High |
|------------|-----|----------|------|
| Audit log review frequency | Weekly | At least weekly | Real-time/continuous |
| Automated analysis | Not required | Required (AU-6(1)) | Required with correlation (AU-6(1)(3)) |
| Centralized analysis | Not required | Required (AU-6(4)) | Required with integrated analysis (AU-6(5)(6)) |
| Reporting | Monthly | Weekly + on-demand | Daily + real-time alerting |

### IR-4: Incident Handling

**Implementation Requirements**:

1. **Preparation**
   - Documented incident response plan (IR-8)
   - Defined incident categories aligned with US-CERT categories
   - Established communication channels with FedRAMP PMO and agency customers
   - Incident handling tools and forensic capabilities deployed

2. **Detection and Analysis**
   - Automated correlation of audit records with incident indicators
   - Integration with US-CERT and agency threat intelligence feeds
   - Defined alert thresholds and escalation criteria

3. **Containment, Eradication, and Recovery**
   - Pre-authorized containment actions for common incident types
   - Documented eradication procedures by incident category
   - Recovery validation and monitoring procedures

4. **Post-Incident Activity**
   - Lessons learned within 5 business days of incident closure
   - Update IR plan based on lessons learned
   - Trend analysis across incidents

### IR-6: Incident Reporting

**FedRAMP Incident Reporting Requirements**:

```
Incident Detected
  |
  +-- Within 1 hour --> Notify agency ISSO and Authorizing Official
  |
  +-- Within 1 hour --> Report to US-CERT (https://us-cert.cisa.gov)
  |                     Use US-CERT incident reporting form
  |                     Category based on US-CERT taxonomy
  |
  +-- Same business day --> Notify FedRAMP PMO (info@fedramp.gov)
  |
  +-- Within 3 days --> Provide detailed incident report
  |                     Include: scope, impact, timeline, IOCs,
  |                     containment actions, root cause (if known)
  |
  +-- Ongoing --> Status updates every 3 business days until closed
  |
  +-- Within 30 days --> Final incident report with root cause,
                          remediation, and lessons learned
```

### SI-4: Information System Monitoring

**Monitoring Implementation**:

| Monitoring Type | Low | Moderate | High |
|----------------|-----|----------|------|
| Intrusion detection | Basic IDS | IDS/IPS with automated alerting | Advanced IDS/IPS with automated response |
| Outbound traffic monitoring | Firewall logging | Content inspection + DLP | Full content inspection + DLP + behavioral |
| Endpoint monitoring | Antimalware | EDR with centralized management | EDR + advanced behavioral analysis |
| Network flow analysis | Not required | Required (SI-4(4)) | Required with full packet capture |
| Wireless monitoring | Not required | Required (SI-4(14)) | Required with active countermeasures |
| DNS monitoring | Basic logging | DNS query logging and analysis | DNS sinkholing + query analysis |

### RA-5: Vulnerability Scanning

**FedRAMP Scanning Requirements**:

| Requirement | Specification |
|------------|---------------|
| Scan frequency (OS/infrastructure) | Monthly at minimum |
| Scan frequency (web applications) | Monthly at minimum |
| Scan frequency (databases) | Monthly at minimum |
| High vulnerability remediation | 30 days |
| Moderate vulnerability remediation | 90 days |
| Low vulnerability remediation | 180 days |
| False positive documentation | Required for each false positive claim |
| Scan tool | Must use FedRAMP-approved scanner or equivalent |
| Credentialed scanning | Required for OS and database scans |
| Reporting | Monthly scan results to agency and FedRAMP PMO |

### SC-7: Boundary Protection

**Implementation Requirements**:

1. Monitor and control communications at the external managed interfaces
2. Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible components
3. Connect to external networks only through managed interfaces with boundary protection devices
4. Moderate/High: Deny network traffic by default; allow by exception (SC-7(5))
5. High: Route internal communications traffic to external networks through authenticated proxy servers (SC-7(8))

## Continuous Monitoring (ConMon) Requirements

FedRAMP requires ongoing security monitoring after initial authorization:

### Monthly Deliverables
- Vulnerability scan results (OS, web app, database)
- POA&M updates with remediation status
- Inventory changes (hardware, software, virtual)
- Significant change requests

### Quarterly Deliverables
- Incident response test/exercise results
- Security control assessment (subset of controls)
- Configuration compliance scan results

### Annual Deliverables
- Full security control assessment by 3PAO
- Updated System Security Plan (SSP)
- Penetration test results (Moderate and High)
- Contingency plan test results
- Privacy impact assessment (if applicable)

## SOC Tooling Requirements for FedRAMP

| Capability | Required For | Specification |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| SIEM | All levels | Centralized log collection, correlation, alerting, retention |
| Vulnerability scanner | All levels | Credentialed scanning, compliance benchmarks, API integration |
| IDS/IPS | Moderate and above | Network-based, updated signatures, custom rules capability |
| EDR | Moderate and above | Endpoint visibility, behavioral detection, remote response |
| WAF | Moderate and above (web apps) | OWASP Top 10 protection, custom rules, logging |
| DLP | High | Content inspection for outbound data, policy enforcement |
| SOAR | Recommended | Automated incident workflows, evidence collection, reporting |
| Threat intelligence | High (required), Moderate (recommended) | Feed integration, IOC matching, context enrichment |

## Common FedRAMP Audit Findings

1. **Incomplete audit logging** - Not all required event types are captured. Conduct an audit log gap analysis against AU-2 requirements.

2. **Vulnerability remediation SLA breaches** - High vulnerabilities older than 30 days. Implement automated tracking and escalation for overdue items.

3. **Missing multi-factor authentication** - MFA not enforced for all privileged and remote access. Verify IA-2 enhancements are fully implemented.

4. **Insufficient continuous monitoring** - Monthly deliverables incomplete or delayed. Automate scan scheduling and report generation.

5. **Change management gaps** - Security impact analysis not performed for all changes. Integrate security review into the change advisory board process.

6. **POA&M stagnation** - Remediation items not progressing. Implement executive visibility and escalation for stale POA&M items.

7. **Encryption not FIPS 140-2/3 validated** - Using non-validated cryptographic modules. Verify all encryption uses FIPS-validated modules and document the module versions.

## References

- FedRAMP Security Assessment Framework: https://www.fedramp.gov
- NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-53/rev-5/final
- NIST SP 800-37 Rev 2 (RMF): https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-37/rev-2/final
- US-CERT Incident Reporting: https://us-cert.cisa.gov
- FedRAMP Continuous Monitoring Strategy Guide: https://www.fedramp.gov/documents/
